functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_make_client
documentation:
Makes an AWS S3 client

```
param access_key_id

AWS access key id

param secret_access_key

AWS secret access key

param region_name

AWS service region name

param endpoint_url

AWS service endpoint url

return

context manager which wraps the AWS S3 client instance
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_list_objects
documentation:
Lists all objects from the given bucket and prefix

```
param client

AWS S3 client instance

param bucket

bucket name

param prefix

object keys prefix

param limit

maximum count of objects returns

return

list of object meta items
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_listfile
documentation:
Lists all objects from the given bucket and prefix

```
param client

AWS S3 client instance

param bucket

bucket name

param prefix

object keys prefix

param include_patterns

inclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param exclude_patterns

exclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param depth

maximum depth of the subdirectories included in the scan

return

list of object meta items
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_cp_download
documentation:
Downloads object from the given bucket and key to the given local file path

```
param client

AWS S3 client instance

param bucket

bucket name

param key

object key

param file_path

local file path
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_cp_upload
documentation:
Uploads object of the given local file path to the given bucket and key

```
param client

AWS S3 client instance

param file_path

local file path

param bucket

bucket name

param key

object key
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_sync_download
documentation:
Recursively downloads all objects from the given bucket and prefix to the given local directory path

```
param client

AWS S3 client instance

param bucket

bucket name

param prefix

object keys prefix

param dir_path

local directory path

param max_workers

number of maximum workers in thread pool

param include_patterns

inclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param exclude_patterns

exclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param depth

maximum depth of the subdirectories included in the scan
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_sync_upload
documentation:
Recursively uploads all objects from the given local directory path to the given bucket and prefix

```
param client

AWS S3 client instance

param dir_path

local directory

param bucket

bucket name

param prefix

object keys prefix

param max_workers

number of maximum workers in thread pool

param include_patterns

inclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param exclude_patterns

exclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param depth

maximum depth of the subdirectories included in the scan
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_pull_text
documentation:
Pulls text stored as object from the given bucket and key

```
param client

AWS S3 client instance

param bucket

bucket name

param key

key of the object storing the text

param encoding

string encoding

return

text content
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.s3utils.s3_push_text
documentation:
Pushes the given text to the object stored by the given bucket and key

```
param client

AWS S3 client instance

param text

text content

param bucket

bucket name

param key

key of the object storing the text

param encoding

string encoding
```

methods.name: iker.common.utils.retry.Attempt.__init__
documentation:
Represents an attempt in retrying

```
param number

attempt number

param prev_wait

previous wait time

param next_wait

next wait time

param start_time

first attempt start time

param check_time

this attempt check time

param last_exception

exception happened in the last attempt
```

methods.name: iker.common.utils.retry.Retry.on_attempt
documentation:
Updates the retry state on attempt

```
param attempt

attempt
```

methods.name: iker.common.utils.retry.RetryWrapper.__init__
documentation:
Retry executor

```
param cls

target callable

param wait

fixed wait between retrials

param wait_exponent_init

initial of exponentially increasing wait

param wait_exponent_max

max of exponentially increasing wait

param wait_random_min

min of random wait

param wait_random_max

max of random wait

param retrials

max retrials

param timeout

timeout
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.extension
documentation:
Extracts filename extension of the given filename or path

```
param filename

the specific filename or path

return

filename extension
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.stem
documentation:
Extracts filename stem of the given filename or path

```
param filename

the specific filename or path

param minimal

True if the minimal (shortest) stem is extracted

return

filename stem
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.extensions
documentation:
Extracts all filename extensions and compound extensions of the given filename or path

```
param filename

the specific filename or path

return

list of all extensions ordered from the shortest to the longest
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.expanded_path
documentation:
Returns absolute expanded path whose environment vars and home tilde has been expanded

```
param path

the given path

return

the absolute canonical path
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.path_depth
documentation:
Returns the relative path depth from the given child to the root

```
param root

the root path

param child

the child path

return

relative depth
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.glob_match
documentation:
Applies the given inclusive and exclusive glob patterns on the given names and returns the filtered result

```
param names

names to apply the glob patterns

param include_patterns

inclusive glob patterns

param exclude_patterns

exclusive glob patterns

return

filtered names
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.listfile
documentation:
Recursively scans the given path and returns list of files whose names satisfy the given name patterns and the
relative depth of their folders to the given root path is not greater than the specific depth value

```
param path

the root path which is scanned

param include_patterns

inclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param exclude_patterns

exclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param depth

maximum depth of the subdirectories included in the scan

return

```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.copy
documentation:
Recursively copies the given source path to the destination path. Only copies the files whose names satisfy the
given name patterns and the relative depth of their folders to the given source path is not greater than the
specific depth value

```
param src

the source path or file

param dst

the destination path or file

param include_patterns

inclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param exclude_patterns

exclusive glob patterns applied to the filenames

param depth

maximum depth of the subdirectories included in the scan

param follow_symlinks

True to create symbolic links for the symbolic links present in the source, otherwise, make
```

a physical copy
\:param ignore_dangling_symlinks: True to ignore errors if the file pointed by the symbolic link does not exist
\:param dirs_exist_ok: True to ignore errors if the destination directory and subdirectories exist
\:param copy_func: copy function

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.run
documentation:
Runs given command and returns the success status

```
param cmd

command to run

return

True if the command has been successfully run
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.shutils.execute
documentation:
Executes given command and returns contents collected from standard output

```
param cmd

command to execute

param strip

True if the contents will be stripped

return

the content from standard output
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.dtutils.dt_utc_now
documentation:
Returns instance of current date time in UTC

```
return

current date time in UTC
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.dtutils.dt_utc
documentation:
Returns instance of the specified date time in UTC

```
param year

year part

param month

month part

param day

day part

param hour

hour part

param minute

minute part

param second

second part

param microsecond

microsecond part

return

specified date time in UTC
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.dtutils.dt_to_ts
documentation:
Returns corresponding timestamp in seconds of the given datetime

```
param dt

the given datetime

return

timestamp in seconds from POSIX epoch
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.dtutils.dt_from_ts
documentation:
Returns corresponding datetime in UTC of the given timestamp in seconds

```
param ts

timestamp in seconds from POSIX epoch

return

the given datetime in UTC
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.dtutils.dt_parse
documentation:
Null safely parses given string value of a datetime to the datetime instance

```

string value of the given datetime

param fmt_str

format string, or a list/tuple of format string candidates

return

datetime instance
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.dtutils.dt_format
documentation:
Null safely formats the given datetime to a string value

```
param dt

the given datetime

param fmt_str

format string

return

string value of the given datetime
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.span.spans_union
documentation:
Computes the union of the given span lists. The spans in each of the lists must be sorted and do not
mutually overlap

```
param a

the first span list

param bs

the remaining span lists

return

union of the span lists whose spans are sorted
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.span.spans_intersect
documentation:
Computes the intersection of the given span lists. The spans in each of the lists must be sorted and do not
mutually overlap

```
param a

the first span list

param bs

the remaining span lists

return

intersection of the span lists whose spans are sorted
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.span.spans_subtract
documentation:
Computes the subtraction on the first span list by the remaining span lists. The spans in each of the
lists must be sorted and do not mutually overlap

```
param a

the first span list

param bs

the remaining span lists

return

subtraction on the first span list by the remaining span lists whose spans are sorted
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.sequtils.head
documentation: See Haskell's list operations head, tail, init, and last
which is also provided in Scala list operations

functions.name: iker.common.utils.sequtils.grouped
documentation:
Groups the given list of elements according to key generator function

```
param ms

list of elements

param key_func

key generator function

param keys_ordered

True if the return elements are sorted according to the keys

param values_only

True if only return elements groups without corresponding keys

return

grouped elements, with corresponding keys if  is set to False
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.sequtils.deduped
documentation:
Dedupes the given list of elements

```
param ms

list of elements

param comp_func

comparator generator function

return

deduped elements
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.sequtils.batch_yield
documentation:
Splits the given input sequence into batches according to the specific batch size

```
param ms

sequence of elements

param batch_size

batch size

return

batches of sequences
```

functions.name: iker.common.utils.sequtils.chunk
documentation:
Chops the list of elements into chunks

```
param ms

list of elements

param chunk_func

chunk generator function with compares the current chunk and the next element from the list

param exclusive_end

set to true to make each chunk (except the last one) carrying the first element of
```

the next chunk as an exclusive end
\:return: list of element chunks

functions.name: iker.common.utils.sequtils.merge_chunks
documentation:
Merges chunks according to the given merging criteria

```
param chunks

chunks to be merged into larger ones

param merge_func

merged chunk generator function

param drop_exclusive_end

set to true if each of the given chunk (except the last one) as an exclusive end element,
```

and these exclusive end elements will be dropped while merging their chunks to the corresponding next chunks
\:return: merged chunks
